description: The point in time or space at which something starts; the first part. 'Thorui' names the opening—where journeys commence, where stories start, where change initiates.
sound_symbolism: The word opens outward. 'Tho' /θo/ begins with breath released, start forming. 'Ru' rolls into motion, movement beginning. Finally 'i' rises with fresh energy, beginning alive.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun with a possessor before it: 'nophi thorui' (the story's beginning). Opposite of 'lumae' (end). Related structurally to 'kesho' (middle).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Beginnings hold possibility—the start of new systems, new relationships, new ways of living.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Every moment is a beginning—the fresh start available now, not dependent on calendar or circumstance.
